findings.category: A01 Broken Access Control / A04 Insecure Design
findings.title: Pre-setup admin-account hijack window — setup token removed
findings.description: POST /api/setup/admin-password is unauthenticated and, with the setup token removed (trinity-enterprise#49), has no proof-of-control during the pre-setup window. A remote attacker reaching the endpoint before the operator completes setup can create the admin account with attacker-controlled credentials.
findings.exploit_scenario: Internet-reachable host, setup not yet completed -> attacker POSTs valid email+password to /api/setup/admin-password -> admin created for attacker, setup_completed=true, operator locked out, attacker holds full admin.
findings.impact: Full administrative control of the instance during the pre-setup window on an internet-reachable deployment.
mitigations: Documented operator responsibility to network-restrict (tunnel/VPN) until setup completes (docs/DEPLOYMENT.md Security Recommendations, setup.py docstrings, main.py startup log, architecture.md, requirements.md)
Endpoint self-disables after first success (setup_completed gate) — exposure limited to pre-setup window
findings.decision_rationale: Operator explicitly chose 'accept + document' over the localhost-skip and host-marker alternatives to remove first-run friction for the common self-hosted single-operator case.
residual_risk_options: Auto-skip token only for loopback/RFC1918, keep for public IPs
Replace token with a host marker file (proof-of-control)

false_positives_dropped: CSS mask-image lines matched secret regex
